subdomain: The Universe and the Solar System
description: Describing the changes in the apearance of the mon during its revolution around the Earth and identifying the phenomena asociated with that.

subdomain: The Universe and the Solar System
description: Explaing the phenomena related to the movement of the earth, the mon and the sun and the resulting changes.

subdomain: The Universe and the Solar System
description: Concluding the efect of gravity on the movement of the solar system, galaxies and asociated phenomena.

subdomain: The Universe and the Solar System
description: Determing the characteristics of the solar system, and comparing the solar system to the galaxy and the universe.

subdomain: Structure and Function in Living Organisms
description: Describing the cel structures and linking them to their vital functions.

subdomain: Structure and Function in Living Organisms
description: Identifying the main body systems and their specialized organs and linking them to their functions that suports growth and survival of living organisms (plants and animals).

subdomain: Structure and Function in Living Organisms
description: Identifying the major structural and functional diferences betwen animal and plant cels.

subdomain: Structure and Function in Living Organisms
description: Describing the diferent paterns in the life cycles of animals and plants and the changes acompanying them, and comparing them.

subdomain: Organization and Diversity of Living Organisms
description: Clasifying living organisms into groups based on comon phenotypic traits.

Describing the efect of environmental changes on plants and animals that live in specific environments. Infering how behavioral and structural adaptations can help plants and animals survive in their habitats

Representing biological comunities and identifying types of population that lives in it, describing the interelationships among them, and their interaction with the abiotic components, and the impact of biological comunities changes on their survival and sustainability.

Describing the components of the ecosystem and explaing the impact of the availability of diferent resources in ecosystems on the survival and sustainability of living organisms, and proposing solutions to problems that afect the stability of the ecosystem.
Representing the relationships betwen living organisms in which mater is circulated in the ecosystem, and identifying the relationship betwen plants and energy obtained from the sun to produce fod.
Infering the efects of human activity on environmental habitats and populations, and predicting its impact, proposing solutions to protect them.

Identifying the inheritance of traits, explaing the variation in them, tracing their transmision from one generation to the next, distinguishing their types (dominant and recesive), and clarifying the impact of the environment on them.
Exploring the physical properties of mater, distinguishing the molecular structure of its diferent states, and explaing the change of mater states due to heat.

Understanding the chemical changes of mater, clarifying the concepts and methods related to it, and comparing mas of diferent maters when their properties change based on the law of conservation of mas.
Understanding chemical reactions, the indicators of their ocurence, types, and factors afecting their reaction rate.
Exploring the chemical properties of materials, and distinguishing betwen the interactions of acids and bases, their chemical properties, and their uses.
Describing the efect of force on objects, and distinguishing types of forces
Explaing the factors afecting types of forces, such as gravitational force, friction, and magnetism.
Understanding Newton’s thre laws of motion and using them to explain body motion.
Understanding the concept of energy and work, distinguishing betwen them, and giving real life examples.
Understanding the principle of energy conservation during its transformations and aply it in daily life.
Understanding the concepts of reflection and refraction of light, transmision of sound, and explaing their role interaction and comunication in the surounding environment.
Describing waves, distinguishing betwen their properties theoreticaly and graphicaly, and predicting their movement.
Understanding the concept of electric charge, explaining the attraction and repulsion of charged bodies, and comparing electrical circuits connected in series and in parallel.
Understanding the properties of magnets and their uses in daily life.
Deducing the relationship of the Earth’s spheres to each other and predicting the interactions, and changes that ocur betwen them and the geological efects resulting from them.
Clasifying the layers of the atmosphere and identifying their components, characteristics, changes, its efects on the environment and their benefits to humans.
Describing the factors and proceses that afected the Earth’s surface which changed some of its features.
Describing the types of rocks and their relationship to minerals and distinguishing their characteristics and uses.
Determing the causes and efects of earthquakes and volcanoes and identifying the sites most vulnerable to earthquakes and volcanoes.
